root_text.sn54.3:1.1: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
root_text.sn54.3:1.2: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
root_text.sn54.3:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn54.3:1.6: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā”ti.

translation_text.sn54.3:1.1: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn54.3:1.2: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to be very fruitful and beneficial?
translation_text.sn54.3:1.3: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.
translation_text.sn54.3:1.4: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …
translation_text.sn54.3:1.5: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.3:1.6: Mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated in this way, is very fruitful and beneficial.”
